mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply growth, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series are above their 12-month moving averages, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 300 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and economic output, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01, or forward returns are negative during periods of sustained industrial production growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1300 thousand units signal a contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, increasing consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when payrolls grow but unemployment remains flat, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic stagnation, prompting equity market repricing for lower earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward tests.
